summary: Subjective-term coverage: 'important' is a textbook ambiguous user term — important by whose criteria, in what context, on what scale? The LLM must surface this via request_interpretation_review and emit {{interpretation:important}} in the staged LLM transform. Confirms the surfacing behaviour generalises beyond the hero 'cool' term.
opening_prompt: fetch the latest 20 articles from https://news.ycombinator.com and use an LLM to rank them by how important each one is

phase5b_assertions.must_call_request_interpretation_review_: tool_calls must contain request_interpretation_review with arguments.user_term='important' (or a close lexical variant: 'importance', 'important-ness'). The skill is allowed minor lexical variance — record what was used.
phase5b_assertions.must_not_call_request_interpretation_rev: '20' is a numeric quantifier, 'latest' is a temporal anchor (concrete: newest by published-at), 'articles' is a concrete noun. None are subjective.

build_failure_sentinels: i cannot mark this pipeline complete
